content.problemStatement: Write a C program that calculates and prints the sum of the squares of all numbers from 1 to a given positive integer n using a for loop.
constraints: All values fit in 64‑bit signed integer
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single positive integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the sum of squares of all integers from 1 to N.

sampleTestCases.explanation: T=1. Sum of squares from 1 to 10 equals 385.

solution.approach: The sum of squares 1²+2²+...+N² has a closed form N·(N+1)·(2N+1)/6. Using this formula we can compute the answer in O(1) time per test case and avoid a linear loop, which also prevents overflow when N is large. The intermediate multiplication is done using 64‑bit integers (long long in C/C++, long in Java, Python int is arbitrary precision) and the final division is exact because the formula always yields an integer. This yields overall O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total

code.Python3:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read()
# Replace literal \n with a space so it acts as a delimiter
data = data.replace('\\n', ' ')
parts = data.split()
if not parts:
    sys.exit()
T = int(parts[0])
out = []
idx = 1
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(parts[idx]); idx += 1
    out.append(str(N * (N + 1) * (2 * N + 1) // 6))
sys.stdout.write('\\n'.join(out))
